channel.title: CyberNetSec - Latest Cybersecurity Articles
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity threat intelligence articles from the past 3 days

item.title: Vulnerabilities Surge 51% as AI-Driven Supply Chain Attacks Rise
item.description:
A Forescout report for H1 2026 reveals a 51% surge in published vulnerabilities and a 25% increase in claimed ransomware attacks. The study highlights the growing abuse of AI by threat actors to accelerate campaigns, the continued risk of sophisticated supply chain attacks, and the persistent target...

Severity: High

Categories: Threat Intelligence, Vulnerability, Supply Chain Attack

Read Full Article →

item.title: Q2 Ransomware Attacks Up 3%; VPNs Remain Top Initial Access Vector
item.description:
According to NCC Group's Q2 2026 threat report, global ransomware attacks increased by 3% to 2,229 incidents. The industrial sector was most targeted, and North America remained the most impacted region. For the fifth straight quarter, the Qilin ransomware group was the most active. The report criti...

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Vulnerability, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: HollowGraph Malware Uses Microsoft 365 Calendar for Covert C2
item.description:
A sophisticated espionage implant named HollowGraph is using Microsoft 365 calendar events for command-and-control (C2) and data exfiltration. The malware, discovered by Group-IB, hijacks a compromised user's calendar and hides operator instructions and stolen data within events dated to the year 20...

Severity: High

Categories: Malware, Cyberattack, Cloud Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: CISA Adds Four Actively Exploited Flaws in DD-WRT, Langflow, WordPress
item.description:
On July 21, 2026, the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) added four new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, indicating they are under active attack. The flaws include a buffer overflow in DD-WRT (CVE-2021-27137), an untrusted control sphere...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management, Security Operations

Read Full Article →

item.title: Manufacturing Sector Faces Precise, Targeted Attacks, SonicWall Reports
item.description:
A SonicWall report finds that while overall cyberattack volume on the manufacturing sector has dropped, attackers are now using more precise, targeted strikes against the convergence of IT and OT networks. The sector recorded the highest rate of SCADA attacks and saw significant exploit activity aga...

Severity: High

Categories: Industrial Control Systems, Cyberattack, Vulnerability

Read Full Article →

item.title: Data-Wiping Attacks on the Rise, Abusing Legitimate Admin Tools
item.description:
The supply chain industry is being warned about a rising trend of destructive data-wiping attacks. A key example is the March 2026 incident at medical tech firm Stryker, where the Iranian hacktivist group Handala Hack Team claimed to have used Microsoft Intune to issue remote wipe commands to 80,000...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Cyberattack, Threat Actor, Cloud Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: Alkegen Data Breach Exposes Social Security Numbers, Health Records
item.description:
Specialty materials manufacturer Alkegen (formerly ASP Unifrax Holdings) has disclosed a data breach that may have exposed the Social Security numbers and personal health records of affected individuals. The incident, first claimed by the Akira ransomware group on April 23, 2026, allegedly involved ...

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Ransomware, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: Supply Chain Attacks Hit 26% of UK Businesses in the Last Year
item.description:
A survey by Databarracks reveals that one in four (26%) UK businesses have suffered a cyber incident originating from their supply chain in the past 12 months. The 'Data Health Check 2026' report highlights a concerning trend: 48% of IT decision-makers admit to knowingly working with suppliers that ...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Policy and Compliance, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: Cisco Releases Open-Weight 'Antares' AI Models for Code Analysis
item.description:
Cisco has released Antares, a family of open-weight small language models (SLMs) specifically trained to perform static application security testing (SAST) and identify security vulnerabilities in source code. The models, including Antares-1.3B and Antares-7B, are designed to run locally on standard...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Security Operations, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: Exposed Server Reveals Attacker's AI-Powered Phishing Toolkit
item.description:
Researchers at Rapid7 discovered an unsecured server that exposed a sophisticated, AI-assisted toolkit for phishing and malware delivery. The operator, believed to be Russian-speaking, was targeting Windows users in Mexico with an infostealer. The toolkit shows extensive use of Large Language Models...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Phishing, Malware, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: China-Aligned APTs Use Public AI Tools in Espionage Campaigns
item.description:
State-sponsored threat actors aligned with China are integrating publicly available AI tools, such as Anthropic's Claude Code and DeepSeek, into their cyber-espionage campaigns. A report from Hunt.io details how the APT groups used these tools as core components to automate attacks, generate malicio...

Severity: High

Categories: Threat Actor, Threat Intelligence, Cyberattack

Read Full Article →

item.title: 'SleeperGem' Supply Chain Attack Hits RubyGems via Dormant Accounts
item.description:
A sophisticated supply chain attack named 'SleeperGem' has targeted the RubyGems ecosystem by hijacking dormant maintainer accounts. Attackers published malicious versions of at least three gems, including the popular 'fastlane-plugin-run_tests_firebase_testlab', to distribute a persistent backdoor....

Severity: High

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Malware, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: Healthcare Software Firm Craneware Hit by Data Breach, Data Stolen
item.description:
Craneware, a UK-based software supplier for over 2,000 US hospitals, has confirmed it suffered a cyberattack resulting in a significant data breach. The company announced on July 20, 2026, that an unauthorized third party exfiltrated a 'significant volume' of data, including records belonging to emp...

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Cyberattack, Supply Chain Attack

Read Full Article →

item.title: CKR Consulting Engineers Hit by 'payload' Ransomware Group
item.description:
The U.S.-based firm CKR Consulting Engineers has been listed as a victim by the 'payload' ransomware group. The data breach was discovered and reported on the group's leak site on July 19, 2026. This incident highlights the ongoing threat of ransomware to professional services firms, which often hol...

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Data Breach, Cyberattack

Read Full Article →

item.title: Oracle Announces July 2026 Critical Patch Update with 1,455 Fixes
item.description:
Oracle has issued a pre-release announcement for its July 2026 Critical Patch Update (CPU), scheduled for July 21. The update is exceptionally large, containing 1,455 new security patches across a vast array of Oracle products. Many of the vulnerabilities are remotely exploitable without authenticat...

Severity: High

Categories: Patch Management, Vulnerability

Read Full Article →

item.title: Governments Move Toward Banning Ransomware Payments Amid Rising Attacks
item.description:
As ransomware attacks become more frequent and sophisticated, governments globally are considering and implementing bans on ransom payments to cybercriminals. A July 20, 2026 report highlights a planned U.K. ban for public sector and critical infrastructure organizations as a key example of this gro...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Ransomware

Read Full Article →

item.title: ReliaQuest and OpenAI Partner to Advance AI in Cyber Defense
item.description:
Cybersecurity firm ReliaQuest announced on July 19, 2026, that it is joining the OpenAI Daybreak Cyber Partner Program. This partnership aims to accelerate the development of advanced AI capabilities for enterprise cyber defense, providing ReliaQuest with access to OpenAI's frontier models to integr...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Security Operations, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: Warning: Free VPN Browser Extensions Caught Harvesting Clipboard Data
item.description:
A warning has been issued about free VPN extensions for Chrome and Firefox that are secretly harvesting sensitive user data from the clipboard. These malicious extensions, masquerading as privacy tools, can steal passwords, financial information, and cryptocurrency keys that users copy and paste. Th...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Malware, Data Breach

Read Full Article →

item.title: Report: Enterprises Overwhelmed by Disjointed Security Tools Amid AI Risks
item.description:
A report from Thales reveals that enterprises are using a multitude of separate security tools, leading to policy inconsistencies and visibility gaps. This 'tool sprawl,' where organizations use an average of seven different tools for data protection alone, is creating a significant and expanding at...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Security Operations, Cloud Security, Policy and Compliance

Read Full Article →
